name: Sync-o-tron

consumes.minimum: 1 active comp + 1 selected layer + 1 selected property + 1 comp whose name contains 'AUDIO REACTOR' in the project
consumes.description: Reads app.project.activeItem (must be a CompItem), comp.selectedLayers[0], layer.selectedProperties to identify the property type and address, and iterates app.project items to find any CompItem whose name contains 'AUDIO REACTOR' for the dropdown.

controls.name: dropdown
controls.role: selects the target AUDIO REACTOR composition from the project

controls.name: tuneSyncButton
controls.role: triggers the TuneSync operation — adds effect controls and writes expression to the selected property

controls.name: refreshButton
controls.role: repopulates the dropdown with current AUDIO REACTOR compositions from the project

controls.name: X checkbox (XY dialog)
controls.role: enables audio-reactive mapping on the X axis for 2D properties

controls.name: Y checkbox (XY dialog)
controls.role: enables audio-reactive mapping on the Y axis for 2D properties

controls.name: Unify Properties checkbox (XY dialog)
controls.role: when checked, applies a single Min/Max Output slider pair to both X and Y axes

controls.name: X checkbox (XYZ dialog)
controls.role: enables audio-reactive mapping on the X axis for 3D properties

controls.name: Y checkbox (XYZ dialog)
controls.role: enables audio-reactive mapping on the Y axis for 3D properties

controls.name: Z checkbox (XYZ dialog)
controls.role: enables audio-reactive mapping on the Z axis for 3D properties

controls.name: Unify Properties checkbox (XYZ dialog)
controls.role: when checked, applies a single Min/Max Output slider pair to all three axes

functions: populate dropdown with all AUDIO REACTOR compositions found in the project
detect whether the selected AE property is 1D, 2D, 3D, or Color (4D)
add an ADBE Dropdown Control effect (renamed '<propName>_Easing Type') to the selected layer
add ADBE Slider Control effects for Min Output / Max Output (per-axis or unified) to the selected layer
add ADBE Color Control effects for Start Color and End Color when syncing a Color property
build and apply an AE expression that maps Audio Reactor output power to the target property value using linear, easeIn, easeOut, or ease interpolation
wrap all mutations in a single undo group named 'Create Controls'
guard against missing active composition, missing selected layer, and missing selected property

scriptSim.scenario: success — sync 1D property (Opacity) to audio reactor
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Main Comp' with 'Shape Layer 1' selected; Opacity property selected; project contains 'AUDIO REACTOR - Kick' comp; dropdown shows 'AUDIO REACTOR - Kick'

sim.note: Sweeps the Audio Reactor Output Power (d) from 0 to 100; with outMin=0 and outMax=100 and easeType=1 (linear), the result maps directly to the Opacity value in the same range.

Sweeps Audio Reactor Output Power (d) from 0 to 100 with the unified 2D path (isUnified=true); both xResult and yResult follow the same eased curve. outMin=0, outMax=500 represents a representative position range.

Sweeps Audio Reactor Output Power (d) from 0 to 100 with the unified 3D path (xSelected=true, ySelected=true, zSelected=false, isUnified=true); x and y follow the eased curve while z holds its original value.

Sweeps Audio Reactor Output Power (d) from 0 to 100; with easeType=1 (linear), interpolates between startColor [0,1,0,1] (green) and endColor [0,0,1,1] (blue) — propName resolved from a Fill effect's Color property (parentName='Fill', propName='Color').

notes: The script runs as a dockable palette (thisObj pattern); it can be run as a floating window or docked into the AE UI.
The Audio Reactor template composition must already exist in the project and contain a layer named exactly 'Select Frequency' that has an effect named 'Audio Reactor' with an 'Output Power' output.
For 1D properties the script uses findPropertyByAddress() (regex-based address string parsing) to locate the target property; for 2D Color and 3D properties it uses findPropertyByMatchName().
PropQuery detects the property type by temporarily setting test expressions on the property and checking expressionError; it restores the original expression afterward.
Names containing 'Vertex' are automatically renamed to 'Property-Vert' in the generated effect control names to avoid AE pseudo-effect conflicts.
The 2D dialog (XYUnifyDialog) exposes X / Y / Unify checkboxes; without any check the script still creates per-axis X and Y sliders (both unselected = treat as independent).
The 3D expression uses easeInOut as the fallback (easeType else branch) while the 1D and 2D expressions use ease — these are different AE functions.
All three slider/dropdown effects are added to layer.Effects (the effect-parade), not to the actual target property.
